name: Cade Otton
headline: Steady volume tight end with thin scoring upside
body:
Otton has quietly become a fixture in Tampa Bay's passing game, playing 93.5% of snaps and running a route on 89.2% of dropbacks in 2025. He drew 81 targets for a 15% target share, hauled in 59 balls, and dropped just one. The problem is the ceiling: only one touchdown despite six red-zone looks, a 5.17 air-yards-per-target figure, and 1.08 yards per route run. His EPA per play slipped from 0.144 in 2024 to 0.05, and his fantasy output cratered from 81.6 points to 63.2 as the scoring dried up.

There's a path back toward his 2024 form. With Mike Evans gone, vacated red-zone work could land on Otton, who tallied three scores across four games when Tampa's top wideouts missed time in 2024. But he's a high-floor, low-spike piece in a crowded room, finishing TE25 last season. Treat him as a streaming or bye-week answer, not a weekly starter.
